title: Measurements of the 128Te(n,2n)127m,gTe reaction cross sections and isomeric cross section ratio of 127m,gTe at the neutron energy of 14 MeV

x4freetext: cross sections and isomeric cross section ratio of
127m,gTe at the neutron energy of 14 MeV

x4freetext: The neutron yield was (4-5)E10 n/s. In the
case of deuterium beams, the average energy value was
135 keV, and the average intensity value was 240 uA.
The thickness of the tritium-titanium was 2.65 mg/cm2.
The radiation lasted for two to three hours.
For this experiment, two measurements were taken
from each sample. The second data-set was taken 4.7 d
later, nearly 12 times the half-life of the ground
state of 127gTe, and the first set was taken between
0.5 and 27 h after irradiation. t1 was cooling time,
no.    T(s)      t1(s)  t2(s)      t1(s)   t2(s)
The activated sample was monitored using an
HPGe detector (ORTEC, model GEM 60P). For 60Co, the
detector`s efficiency was 68%, and its energy
natural isotopic composition weigh between 3.4 and
4.1 g and are 99.99% refined. The sample was a disc
with a diameter of 20 mm. Five samples and 10
monitors made of 99.99% pure niobium foil and 99%
pure zirconium with the same diameter were
No.  Mass   Thickness  128Te weight   Number of atoms
Self-absorption correction factors for the five Te
